license_policy.default_content_license: Original repo-authored course design; source text must use public-domain or explicitly licensed editions before inclusion.
license_policy.source_text_boundary: This metadata references great works but does not include copyrighted translations or copied course assets.

source_canon.author: Modern behavior-change, motivation, coaching, social influence, habit, and adult-learning researchers
source_canon.work: Motivational interviewing, readiness-to-change, self-determination, self-efficacy, implementation intentions, habit design, cognitive dissonance, attitude-change, reactance, diffusion, and adult-learning research
source_canon.role: Helping people see the need for change, own the reason, form a concrete next step, and sustain revision without coercion.
source_canon.recommended_use: change conversations, autonomy-supportive persuasion, implementation planning, relapse repair, and coached practice
source_canon.use_boundary: Reference-only; do not copy manuals, scales, proprietary worksheets, therapy protocols, or copyrighted research text.

advanced_tracks.title: Grammar And Meaning
advanced_tracks.capstone_artifact: Meaning audit of a public argument.

advanced_tracks.title: Rhetoric And Public Speech
advanced_tracks.capstone_artifact: Ethical persuasive speech with rubric and revision.

advanced_tracks.title: Civic And Moral Judgment
advanced_tracks.capstone_artifact: Civic judgment memo with objections and limits.

advanced_tracks.title: Adversarial Reasoning
advanced_tracks.capstone_artifact: Combative exchange repair and self-command audit.

advanced_tracks.title: Change Facilitation
outcomes: help another person see the need for change
turn insight into a concrete next step
advanced_tracks.capstone_artifact: Change conversation plan, transcript or role-play, next-step agreement, and follow-up revision.

advanced_tracks.title: Teacher Formation
advanced_tracks.capstone_artifact: Recorded or transcribed teach-back with correction notes.

practice_labs.title: Source-Move Extraction
practice_labs.evidence: Learner turns a source reference into a repeatable drill without copying source text.

practice_labs.title: Objection Box
practice_labs.evidence: Learner states and answers the strongest objections with concessions.

practice_labs.title: Induction Ladder
practice_labs.evidence: Learner separates observations, rivals, uncertainty, and conclusion.

practice_labs.title: Evidence And Incentive Map
practice_labs.evidence: Learner identifies evidence gaps, incentives, costs, beneficiaries, and limits.

practice_labs.title: Eristic Shield
practice_labs.evidence: Learner handles pressure tactics without returning manipulation.

practice_labs.title: Debate And Persuasion Practice Lab
practice_labs.evidence: Learner stabilizes terms, recognizes disingenuous moves, serves honest listeners, and gives opponents a path to self-revision.

practice_labs.title: Change Facilitation Lab
practice_labs.evidence: Learner identifies readiness, reflects ambivalence, preserves autonomy, builds an implementation plan, and supports relapse repair without coercion.

practice_labs.title: Teach-Back
practice_labs.evidence: Learner teaches a reasoning move, diagnoses confusion, and revises instruction.

modules.title: Attention And Distinction
modules.assessment: Explain three ways one debate fails because categories were mixed.
modules.performance_pressure: Private written analysis and short spoken explanation.

modules.title: Terms, Definitions, And Categories
modules.assessment: Repair a confused argument by stabilizing its key terms.
modules.performance_pressure: Friendly questioner asks for examples and counterexamples.

modules.title: Propositions And Contradiction
modules.assessment: Map a paragraph into propositions and test consistency.
modules.performance_pressure: Confused listener asks whether two claims can both be true.

modules.title: Euclidean Proof Craft
modules.assessment: Reconstruct a simple proof-like argument from accepted premises.
modules.performance_pressure: Learner explains each step aloud without skipping warrant questions.

modules.title: Valid Inference And Syllogism
modules.assessment: Translate a public claim into syllogistic form and test validity.
modules.performance_pressure: Skeptical listener challenges validity and premise truth separately.

modules.title: Demonstration And First Principles
modules.assessment: Write a demonstration-style explanation of a real-world claim.
modules.performance_pressure: Skeptical listener asks what would have to be true first.

modules.title: Dialectic And Ordered Questions
modules.assessment: Conduct a written dialogue that clarifies disagreement.
modules.performance_pressure: Friendly opponent answers unpredictably but cooperatively.

modules.title: Fallacies And Sophistry
modules.assessment: Produce a fallacy repair packet for a combative argument.
modules.performance_pressure: Combative opponent uses insults, topic shifts, and false dilemmas.

modules.title: Rhetoric And Ethical Persuasion
skills: help another person see the need for change without coercion
modules.assessment: Deliver a short speech outline with ethical constraint notes.
modules.performance_pressure: Public audience needs clarity without oversimplification.

modules.title: Dialogue And Disagreement
modules.assessment: Steelman and answer a position the learner strongly rejects.
modules.performance_pressure: Skeptical listener challenges motive and conclusion.

modules.title: Modern Transfer
modules.assessment: Analyze a modern argument and identify what would change the conclusion.
modules.performance_pressure: Learner answers a fast-moving current-events claim without overclaiming.

modules.title: Capstone Performance
modules.assessment: Submit and defend the Clear Reasoning Capstone Portfolio.
modules.performance_pressure: Live disputation, public speech, teach-back, and final self-correction.

required_artifacts: persuasive speech outline and performance rubric
capstone.passing_standard: The learner must demonstrate sound reasoning, fair representation, ethical persuasion, evidence humility, clear speech, teach-back ability, and specific self-correction.
